CompareVenuePlanCockpitDebriefTrim CheckTrim Lab

Debrief

Route — coloured by leg
Speed vs polar — where were we slow? (wind band × point of sail)
Each cell = Little Johnka's boatspeed as a % of her polar target, VMG-matched upwind/down. Green ≥100%, amber 90–100%, red below. The 4–6 kt row (starred, faded) is scored against a modelled light-air polar — low confidence. 6 kt+ rows are the certificate.
By point of sail — certified wind (6–8 kt)
By point of sail — modelled light air (4–6 kt) · low confidence
Scored against the modelled polar, on model wind and SOG. Read the shape, not the number — e.g. an over-100% upwind cell here is wind-model/SOG error, not real speed. This is what the boat's own log will replace first.
Leg splits
Route split — where your path cost you (vs the boats that beat you)
Instrument-log mode — heel/trim cause-finder
This is what the Debrief becomes on the boat's own data: measured STW and true wind, so nothing is model or SOG. Because the DST810 also gives heel and trim, each under-polar cell can be read against how the boat was sitting — the test that separates a trim / crew-weight problem from a helming one.
How to read this
